title: Decadent Smoked Slovakian Lamb Pilaf
description: Rich, snappy Slovakian dish featuring chervil and a miso-marinate.
instructions: Rinse the lamb shoulder under cold running water, then pat dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix together miso paste, smoked paprika, garlic, and 2 tbsp of water to form a smooth paste.
Rub the miso-marinate all over the lamb shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat the ghee or neutral oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb shoulder for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until nicely browned.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast the lamb for 30 minutes.
While the lamb is roasting, heat 2 tbsp of ghee or neutral oil in a separate skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped parsley and chervil to the skillet and cook for 1-2 minutes, or until fragrant.
Fluff the cooked rice with a fork and stir in the stock, Worcestershire sauce, and sautéed onion mixture.
Remove the lamb from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Slice the lamb against the grain and serve on top of the pilaf.
